title: Sticherus yungensis
taxonomy: Sticherus yungensis is a species of fern in the family Gleicheniaceae. This classification places it within the division Pteridophyta, which includes all vascular plants that reproduce via spores. The Gleicheniaceae family is a group of ferns commonly known as water ferns or chain ferns. Sticherus yungensis is native to South America and is found in the mountainous regions of the Andes. Its classification is further grouped into the genus Sticherus, which contains several species of ferns.

intro: Sticherus yungensis, also known as Yung's shield fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Gleicheniaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Sticherus yungensis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
